tickets.title: US001: Register with university email
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to register using my .edu email address, so that the marketplace remains exclusive to university members only.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] System only accepts email addresses ending in .edu
- [ ] A verification email is sent after registration
- [ ] Account is only activated after email is verified

tickets.title: US002: Scan barcode to list item for sale
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to scan a barcode with my phone camera to list an item, so that I can create a listing quickly without manually entering product details.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] App activates camera to scan barcode
- [ ] System automatically fills in item title and details from barcode
- [ ] Student can edit the auto-filled information before publishing

tickets.title: US003: Select Safe Exchange Zone before chatting
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to select a Safe Exchange Zone before chatting with a seller, so that all physical meetups occur in a safe and designated location on campus.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] System displays a list of available Safe Exchange Zones
- [ ] Student cannot open chat without selecting a Safe Exchange Zone
- [ ] Selected zone is visible to both buyer and seller in the chat

tickets.title: US004: Manage Safe Exchange Zone listings
tickets.description:
As an Administrator, I want to manage the list of Safe Exchange Zones, so that students always have access to accurate and up-to-date safe meetup locations on campus.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Administrator can add, edit, or remove Safe Exchange Zones
- [ ] Changes to the list are reflected immediately in the app
- [ ] Each zone includes a name, location description, and availability hours

tickets.title: US005: Auction items to other students
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to auction my items with a start price and end time, so that I can get the best price for my belongings from other students.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Student can set a starting bid price and auction end date
- [ ] Other students can place bids before the auction ends
- [ ] Highest bidder is notified automatically when auction ends
- [ ] Seller is notified of the final winning bid

analysis.ambiguity1:
Requirement: "The app should force users to select a safe zone before they can chat."

Why vague: The term "force" is not defined — it is unclear whether the system should block the chat feature entirely or just display a warning. Additionally, there is no definition of what qualifies as a "Safe Exchange Zone" or who is responsible for maintaining that list.
analysis.ambiguity2:
Requirement: "I want to be able to auction items, not just set a fixed price."

Why vague: The auction mechanism is not defined — there is no specification for minimum bid increment, auction duration, how a winner is determined, or what happens if no bids are placed before the deadline.
analysis.conflictSolution:
Conflict: The University Legal Team states the app cannot process any payments, as the university is not a bank. However, the Student Union President wants to collect a 2% transaction fee to fund club activities.

Solution: The app will not process payments directly. Instead, it will display a suggested voluntary contribution of 2% to the Student Union fund, with payment handled externally via Venmo or cash. This keeps the university legally clear while still supporting club funding goals.
analysis.nfr1: Security: Only users with a valid .edu email address may register. All accounts must be verified before accessing the marketplace to prevent unauthorized external users.
analysis.nfr2: Safety: The system must enforce selection of a Safe Exchange Zone before enabling chat between buyers and sellers to ensure all physical meetups occur in designated safe locations.
analysis.nfr3: Usability: The app must support barcode scanning for listing items, allowing students to create listings quickly without manually entering product details such as ISBN or title.
